next_steps: Send CSF for Angiostrongylus cantonensis PCR
Send serum and CSF for Angiostrongylus cantonensis antibody (ELISA)
Perform comprehensive infectious disease screening of CSF including HSV, VZV, West Nile virus, and mycobacterial cultures
Repeat lumbar puncture if symptoms worsen to assess CSF pressure and for parasite visualization
Consult infectious disease and neurology specialists
Maintain empirical broad-spectrum antibiotic and antiviral coverage until culture results are finalized
Strict monitoring of neurological status and intracranial pressure
Send CSF for Angiostrongylus cantonensis NAAT/PCR testing
Obtain MRI of the brain and spine with and without gadolinium contrast to assess for radiculitis, arachnoiditis, or enhancement patterns
Obtain comprehensive ophthalmologic evaluation to check for intraocular parasites (e.g., Gnathostoma or Angiostrongylus)
Review medication history for potential drug-induced aseptic meningitis (e.g., NSAIDs, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole)
Consult Infectious Disease and Neurology for management of intracranial pressure and potential corticosteroid therapy for helminthic inflammatory response
Monitor neurological status and serial intracranial pressures if the patient remains symptomatic
